qa_pairs.question: Where is the ImageNet dataset available, and what are the requirements for accessing it?
qa_pairs.answer: The ImageNet dataset is available on the Digital Research Alliance of Canada's Nibi cluster, stored in the `/datashare` space. To gain access, users must opt-in by visiting the specified opt-in page and acknowledging that they have registered for an ImageNet license with the data owner (http://image-net.org/download) and have agreed to ImageNet’s terms of use (https://image-net.org/download.php).

qa_pairs.question: What versions of the ImageNet dataset are provided on the Nibi cluster, and which one is most commonly used?
qa_pairs.answer: The Nibi cluster offers several versions of the ImageNet dataset. These include the Full dataset (ImageNet-21k) from Winter 2021, found in `winter21_whole`; the Large-scale Visual Recognition Challenge (LSVRC) 2012, located in `ILSVRC2012` (which is generally the most commonly used version); Tiny ImageNet in `tiny-imagenet-200`; and various downsampled versions (8x8, 16x16, 32x32, and 64x64) available in `/datashare/ImageNet/DownSampled`.
